Fix scroll tracking during falls in climber (#321)

* Fix scroll tracking during falls (#318)

- Player sprite (actor 0) is never hidden by the off-screen check.
  When rel_hi != 0, clamp screen_y to 224 instead of skipping render.
  This matches the original climber.c which uses signed 16-bit math
  for screen_y and never culls the player.

- player_screen_y is always updated for actor 0, even when off-screen,
  so the scroll handler can track the player during falls.

- Scroll handler adjusts 1px/frame (matching original check_scroll_down).

The remaining issue is that on certain randomly-generated floor layouts,
the byte-pair subtraction for rel_hi can produce incorrect results
when yy and scroll_yy have certain page-boundary relationships. This
causes the player to appear at the wrong screen position temporarily.
A proper fix requires the transpiler to support signed 16-bit subtraction
for the screen_y computation, matching the original's int arithmetic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix smoke-test on Linux: use xvfb-run for headless Mesen

Mesen2 is an Avalonia GUI app that requires X11. Ubuntu CI runners
have no display server, causing 'XOpenDisplay failed'. Install xvfb
and wrap the dotnet run command with xvfb-run --auto-servernum.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix smoke-test: remove --no-build so MSBuild evaluates MesenRunArguments

The previous approach used 'dotnet run --no-build' which may not
properly evaluate the -p:MesenRunArguments property, causing --testrunner
to not reach Mesen. Without --testrunner, Mesen initializes Avalonia GUI
which fails on headless CI runners (XOpenDisplay on Linux, hangs on
macOS/Windows waiting for user interaction).

Mesen's --testrunner mode is truly headless (bypasses Avalonia entirely),
so no xvfb needed on any platform. Removed the xvfb step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix smoke tests: invoke Mesen directly, address review comments

- Invoke Mesen binary directly instead of 'dotnet run' with
  MesenRunArguments property. The -p: property wasn't reaching Mesen's
  args, causing it to skip --testrunner and initialize Avalonia GUI
  (XOpenDisplay failure on Linux, hangs on macOS/Windows).
- Remove --no-build from 'dotnet test' per repo convention
- Remove misleading touch step from cache-mesen action
- Use download-artifact@v4 per review suggestion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add MesenTestRunner/MesenTimeout/MesenLuaScript MSBuild properties

Replace the single MesenRunArguments string property with discrete
typed properties that avoid shell word-splitting issues when passed
via 'dotnet run -p:'. Each property maps to specific Mesen CLI flags:

- MesenTestRunner (bool) -> --testrunner --doNotSaveSettings
- MesenTimeout (int) -> --timeout=N
- MesenLuaScript (path) -> appended as Lua script path

This ensures 'dotnet run' correctly passes all flags to Mesen,
validating the full template->transpile->Mesen pipeline in CI.

Also addresses review comments:
- Remove --no-build from dotnet test (repo convention)
- Remove misleading touch step from cache-mesen action
- Use download-artifact@v4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix RunArguments: remove " quotes around ROM path

dotnet run on Linux passes the RunArguments string through .NET's
Process.Start argument parsing, which treats the " around the
ROM filename as part of a quoted string. This causes the entire
RunArguments to be collapsed into fewer args, preventing Mesen
from seeing --testrunner as a separate argument.

Fix: create a minimal settings.json so Mesen skips the wizard and

commit.message:
Fix #380: convert hardcoded addresses to labels, revert #371 (#387)

Two changes:

1. Convert BuiltInSubroutines hardcoded addresses to label references
   - NMI: JMP_abs(0x81E6) -> JMP_abs(nameof(skipAll))
   - IRQ: JMP_abs(skipNtsc) -> JMP_abs(nameof(skipNtsc))
   - PalBright: JSR/JMP using ushort constants -> nameof() labels
   - PpuOff/PpuOnOff: JMP_abs(ppu_wait_nmi) -> label reference
   - Delay: JSR(ppu_wait_nmi) -> label reference
   - DetectNTSC: JSR(0x8280) -> JSR(nameof(ppu_off))
   - DoUpdate: JMP_abs(0x81C0) -> JMP_abs(nameof(updVRAM))
   - UpdVRAM: JSR(0x8383) -> JSR(nameof(updName))
   - ClearRAM: JSR(0x8279/824E/82AE/84F4) -> nameof() labels
   - palBrightTableL/H: split into labeled blocks, add LDA_abs_X(string)
   - FlushVramUpdate: split updName (global, at LDY) and @updLoop
     (local, at LDA) for correct external vs internal jump targets

2. Revert #371 wasUshortInAX guard in WriteLdloc(byte)
   #371 skipped pusha unconditionally when _ushortInAX was previously
   true, but this broke cases where the pusha was needed. Reverting
   restores correct codegen.

All 611 tests pass. cc65 stack verified stable at \ through 6000
frames in Mesen2. Climber game renders and plays correctly.

The climber scroll_yy cleanup (PR #354) is deferred until the
transpiler properly handles (byte)ushort_var patterns in loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: always send form context on button submit

Two root causes fixed:

1. TextField now seeds DataContext on first composition via LaunchedEffect.
   Previously, dataContext.update() only fired in onValueChange, so fields
   never modified by the user had no entry in DataContext. Button would then
   find null at all paths and silently drop the entire context object.

2. resolveActionContext now returns JsonPrimitive("") for paths that resolve
   to null, instead of excluding those keys. This ensures the context object
   is always included in the UserActionEvent even if some fields are empty.
   Cancel button (context=[]) still correctly produces null context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wire form field values to button submit context

[zutils] B: Strip v-prefix from sysroot version when suffixing dependency refs

When NANVIX_VERSION is set to a git tag like "v0.12.291", the
"v" prefix was included in the dependency suffix, producing
"1.3.1-nanvix-v0.12.291". However, release tags use the bare
version without the "v" prefix (e.g., "1.3.1-nanvix-0.12.291").

This caused dependency resolution to fail with HTTP 404 for any
port that declares dependencies in nanvix.toml — sqlite being
the first such port.

The resolver (resolver.py) and resolve command (resolve_cmd.py)
already strip the "v" prefix. This commit aligns manifest.py
with that convention by calling removeprefix("v") before
constructing the suffix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[zutils] B: Route info and success logs to stderr

info() and success() wrote to stdout, which corrupts structured output
when callers pipe stdout to a file (e.g. nanvix-zutil resolve >>
"$GITHUB_OUTPUT"). This breaks CI for any consumer with [dependencies]
in nanvix.toml because the resolver emits info messages during lockfile
downloads that get mixed into the key=value output.

Route both functions to sys.stderr, matching warning(), error(), and
fatal() which already write to stderr. Update tests to capture stderr.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix cc65 stack leak from WriteLdloc(byte) when _ushortInAX was true (#371)

* Initial plan

* Fix cc65 stack leak from (byte)ushort_var patterns when _ushortInAX was true

When WriteLdc(ushort) loaded a 16-bit constant into A:X and the next
instruction loaded a byte local via WriteLdloc, the byte local path
emitted JSR pusha to save the low byte of the ushort. This pusha was
never consumed because:
1. The ushort constant is already tracked on the Stack
2. HandleAddSub uses the Stack operand directly (ADC #lo/ADC #hi)
3. The pusha'd value has no matching popa

Fix: track whether _ushortInAX was true before WriteLdloc clears it.
When loading a byte local after a ushort was in A:X, skip the pusha
emission since the Stack already tracks the value.

Affected samples: climber, peekpoke, battery, irq, shoot2

Replace dotnes.anese with dotnes.mesen emulator (#369)

* Replace dotnes.anese with dotnes.mesen for emulator integration

ANESE is MIT but x86-only (no arm64). Mesen2 is GPLv3, so we can't
bundle binaries. Instead, the dotnes.mesen NuGet package contains only
MSBuild .targets that download Mesen from GitHub releases at build
time and cache it in obj/mesen/. This is not redistribution — the
user's machine downloads directly from the original source.

- Created build/dotnes.mesen.targets with download/unzip + RunCommand
- Simplified dotnes.mesen.csproj to a NuGet package (13KB, no binaries)
- Updated all 45 sample .csproj files: dotnes.anese -> dotnes.mesen
- Updated project template to use dotnes.mesen
- Mesen download skipped on CI (local dev tool only)
- Supports Windows, Linux x64/arm64, macOS x64/arm64

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Cache Mesen in NuGet package dir, remove CI skip logic

- Download to \../bin/ so Mesen is cached
  alongside the NuGet package, shared across all projects
- Remove _SkipMesenDownload — download on CI too
- Delete zip after extraction to save disk space
- Simpler .targets with fewer targets and conditions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add SHA256 hash verification for Mesen downloads

Verify downloaded zip against known SHA256 digests from GitHub's
release asset API. Uses an inline MSBuild task with

Hashes sourced from: gh api repos/SourMesen/Mesen2/releases/tags/2.1.1

* Address review: absolute path for RunCommand, fail-fast Error checks

* Add named mutex to prevent parallel download race condition

CI confirmed the race: multiple samples building in parallel all hit
_MesenDownload simultaneously, causing 'file used by another process'
errors on the shared zip file.

Fix: acquire a Global named mutex before download/unzip. Second project
to acquire the lock re-checks Exists() and skips if already done.

* Use proper MSBuild task instead of inline RoslynCodeTaskFactory

Move download/verify/unzip logic to EnsureMesenInstalled.cs in
dotnes.tasks — a proper compiled MSBuild task with:
- Named mutex for parallel build safety (try/finally guarantees release)
- SHA256 hash verification
- HttpClient for downloads
- ZipFile for extraction

Ship dotnes.tasks.dll in the dotnes.mesen nupkg's build/ folder so
the UsingTask can load it. No more fragile inline C# in XML.

* fix(container): disable BUILD_TESTING in Dockerfile; drop yaml-cpp from spack

include(CTest) implicitly sets BUILD_TESTING=ON, which causes cmake to
require yaml-cpp even though no tests are being built in the container.
Add -DBUILD_TESTING=OFF to avoid this.

yaml-cpp is a test-only dependency and is not present in the EIC spack
buildcache (ghcr.io/eic/spack-v2025.11.0), so spack had to build it
from source — lengthening the base-layer build unnecessarily.  Remove
it from spack.yaml so the base image only contains runtime dependencies
(Geant4 and OpenCASCADE).

* ci(container): cache spack base layer in GHCR by spack.yaml hash

base-container.yml:
- Hash .github/spack.yaml (first 8 hex chars of SHA-256) to produce a
  stable tag spack-<hash> that changes only when the environment changes.
- After GHCR login, inspect ghcr.io/eic/g4occt:spack-<hash> to detect a
  cache hit; skip the 44-min spack install + buildcache push on hit.
- On cache miss, push the new image under the hash tag (not :base directly)
  so the immutable content-addressed entry survives future retagging.
- Always retag the hash-tagged image as :base via docker buildx imagetools
  create (manifest-only, no layer download) so downstream steps find it.
- Add docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 (needed for imagetools create).

container.yml:
- Add cache-from/cache-to (type=registry, :buildcache tag) to
  build-push-action so Docker BuildKit layer cache is stored in GHCR and
  reused across builds when only the G4OCCT source changes.

already used in the byte local path.

1. Add -quiet to run-clang-tidy so the per-file verbose summary
   ('N warnings generated. Suppressed M warnings (N in non-user code
   ...). Use -header-filter=.* to display errors from all non-system
   headers.') is not printed for every analysed file.

2. Pass '^(?!.*_fetchcontent)' as the files-regex argument so that
   source files under _fetchcontent/ (googlebenchmark, googletest) are
   excluded from analysis.  compile_commands.json includes those TUs
   because they are built as part of the project, but they are not our
   code and should not be linted.

The 'Run clang-tidy on changed files' step (PR events) already has
-quiet and only analyses files that appear in the PR diff, so it is
unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(clang-tidy): use -source-filter instead of positional regex

The positional 'files' argument to run-clang-tidy is a regex on the
file path (documented as 'Files to be processed (regex on path)' and
implemented with re.search()), so '^(?!.*_fetchcontent)' as a positional
arg is also correct.  However, the named -source-filter option makes the
intent unambiguous and is less likely to be misread as a literal filename.

-source-filter is applied with re.match() and achieves the same result:
files whose absolute path contains '_fetchcontent' are excluded from
analysis; all other project source files are analysed as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix branch comparison overflow for constants > 255 (#363)

* Initial plan

* Add regression tests for ushort < 256 branch comparison

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jonathanpeppers/dotnes/sessions/c79f24fe-79e4-4155-a495-ca61f5c4b632

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: handle branch comparison overflow gracefully instead of throwing

The Blt, Bge, Bne, Beq branch handlers threw
"Branch comparison value N exceeds byte range" when the comparison
value exceeded 255 and _ushortInAX was false. This could happen
when a ushort local was compared against a constant > 255 (e.g.,
`scroll_yy < 256`).

Now these handlers match the existing Ble and Bgt patterns:
- Blt: byte < 256+ → always true → emit JMP
- Bge: byte >= 256+ → always false → skip (no-op)
- Bne: byte != 256+ → always true → emit JMP
- Beq: byte == 256+ → always false → skip (no-op)
